what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to an external image asset, specifically slide p-09 of presentation-15p, via an image:// URI.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp.
The file body after the frontmatter is empty.
standouts: The target uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme pointing to a specific presentation slide.
It is a metadata-only attachment file with no descriptive body text.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Represents an attachment as a separate markdown file in the channel-memory/attachments directory.
Uses an image:// URI to reference the attached asset.
concerns: No alt text, caption, or description of the image is provided.
No provenance, author, or reason for attachment is recorded.
The image:// URI cannot be resolved without knowledge of an external storage mapping.
There is no version or checksum for the referenced image.
notes: A lightweight, timestamped pointer that attaches slide p-09 of a design presentation to the design-source channel memory.
questions: What visual or semantic content does 'p-09' of 'presentation-15p' contain?
Which channel-memory entry does this attachment belong to?
How is the 'image://' URI resolved to an actual file or storage location?
Why was this particular slide attached at the recorded time?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an image attachment record, and the file does exactly that; they match.
intention_signals: It appears intended to associate a specific design presentation slide image with the design-source channel so the image can be retrieved as contextual material alongside related design records.
novelty: The use of an 'image://' URI scheme to target a presentation slide asset, combined with an empty-body metadata-only attachment record.
contribution: It contributes a stable, timestamped reference from a channel memory entry to an external image asset, allowing visual design context to be fetched alongside related substrates without embedding binary data.
summary_for_embedding: A metadata-only channel-memory attachment file in the design-source channel that references slide p-09 of presentation-15p as an image asset via an image:// URI and records an attachment timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To link a specific design presentation slide image into the design-source channel memory for contextual retrieval.
